rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+) — student leaves chair while agent works
50% magic rule — at least three post-M2 modules produce 'holy shit' moments
Trust the agentic design — no human backstops
Exercise quality ladder — target 8, design 9 for peaks
One exercise = one bounded activity
Modules are composable across training variants
Three-level completeness — trace from strategy frame to runtime artifact to sendable asset
Pattern-recognition LOs need n≥2 instances with contrast
Progression-with-variations — same move, different face per module
Student agency — adults decide; opportunity ≠ compulsion
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Split a mega-exercise when phases are distinct skills with distinct artifacts
Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file must exist and be available
Teach the principle and the technique — default; explain why and how
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
Measure silent failure, not just friction
Verification layers sample different error distributions — not belt-and-braces
Cheap-before-expensive verification-ladder ordering
Cohort-delivered exercises run on trainer-paced concurrent execution, not sequential
Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
Classroom is default; body teaches the work, trainer manages the room — no conversations in body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:47-48: 'If you've run this move before and are working solo, collapse them: paste the first prompt and ask the same conversation to critique before save and invoke after. The three moves don't change.' — Body text authors a conditional delivery instruction ('if working solo') directed at the student's pacing choice; this is cohort-management framing, not the work itself. Also line:25: 'Push back where Claude generalises... re-run the prompt with the quote rule re-asserted.' reads like authored pacing instruction inside body prose rather than a fenced prompt forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The 'if you've run this move before and are working solo, collapse them' sentence addresses self-study pacing; move to maintainer block or wrap as a self-study carve-out blockquote outside the golden path.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report and self-audit charity — verify at artifact, over-flag or fresh-session for critique
Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic
When task selection drives learning outcome, name floor AND ceiling explicitly
Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted
Removals are strategic — ask before cutting
Plug points + UX — file must be both plug-able and operable
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes
Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one recovery move
Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
Save belongs with authoring, not loading
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
Deliberate-antipattern prompts are class-judge exempt — flagged-by-design
Session widget — source shape and placement rule
Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:9: '**What you do:** Diff the packaged M5 re-run against the un-packaged M4 baseline. Name what packaging caught, what it missed, and what new gaps surfaced. Then author a session-shaper skill through conversation. Shape follows what the two runs taught. Self-critique, invoke on the packaged run, ship personally.' — The pre-action framing clause describes the mechanism AND the felt-evidence the prompts will reveal ('Name what packaging caught, what it missed, and what new gaps surfaced'). This previews the discovery the Phase 1 prompt should produce. The three-axis diagnostic: (a) names artifact? Partial. (b) names activity? Yes, but also previews mechanism. (c) names constraint? No. (d) names what prompt will reveal? Yes — 'what packaging caught, what it missed, what new gaps surfaced' is felt-evidence preview.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Trim **What you do:** to artifact + activity + constraint only — e.g. 'Diff the two runs, name the dominant gap, author a second skill from the diagnosis.' Cut the 'what packaging caught, what it missed' mechanism preview.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout, not forcing function
Multi-session ships as packaging, not new primitive
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
Inbound links preserve pedagogical beat across reference restructuring
